ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Tom Yum Beef Noodle Soup. FOODON_00002473 (beef noodle soup food product) is used as the closest parent. The Tom Yum spicy broth character and tendon/brisket components are not captured by this code.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Beef soup) is the closest defensible parent. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for Tom Yum Beef Noodle Soup with brisket and tendon.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Thai beef noodle soup preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving is approximately 500 g, comprising a bowl of spicy Tom Yum broth with beef slices, brisket, tendon, and noodles.

nutrient_source.title: Beef noodle soup, Thai-style — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition tables for Southeast Asian noodle soups
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FAO INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Tom Yum Beef Noodle Soup exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for beef brisket (cooked, braised; FDC ID 174032), beef tendon (cooked), rice noodles (cooked; FDC ID 168917), and a Tom Yum broth profile derived from FAO/INFOODS SEA composition data for spicy Thai broths. Proportions estimated as approximately 30% beef components, 25% noodles, 45% broth by weight. Sodium is elevated due to fish sauce and broth base. Values represent a per-100g composite estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — rice noodles and mixed beef noodle soup analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Tom Yum Beef Noodle Soup. GI estimated at 45 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for rice noodles (GI approximately 40–53, mean ~45) as the primary glycemic driver. The protein and fat content from beef components and the acidic Tom Yum broth (lime juice, lemongrass) are known to attenuate postprandial glycemic response, supporting a low GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (45 × 52.5 g carbohydrate in 500 g serving) / 100 = 23.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce and broth base (~480 mg per 100 g; ~2400 mg per 500 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to very high sodium content from fish sauce and concentrated broth; a single serving may provide approximately 100–120% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load from rice noodles with low GI (~45) partially mitigated by protein, fat, and acidic broth components
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size as a standard 500 g serving delivers approximately 52.5 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~23.6; the low GI of rice noodles and the acidic Tom Yum broth may attenuate the postprandial glucose response, but portion control remains essential.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium content and moderate saturated fat from beef brisket and tendon
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should exercise caution with this dish due to its high sodium content (~2400 mg per serving) and moderate saturated fat from beef brisket and tendon, both of which are associated with elevated blood pressure and adverse lipid profiles.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tom Yum Beef Noodle Soup is a Thai noodle dish featuring a spicy, sour, and aromatic Tom Yum broth served with tender beef slices, slow-braised beef brisket, and gelatinous beef tendon over rice noodles or egg noodles. The Tom Yum broth is prepared by simmering lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, bird's eye chillies, fish sauce, and lime juice to create a complex, intensely flavoured soup base that is simultaneously hot, sour, salty, and slightly sweet. The beef components — sliced beef, brisket, and tendon — are typically braised or simmered until tender, contributing rich umami depth and collagen from the tendon. Per 100 g, the dish provides approximately 98 kcal, 7.8 g protein, 3.2 g fat, and 10.5 g carbohydrate. Sodium content is high at approximately 480 mg per 100 g due to fish sauce and the concentrated broth, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low), driven primarily by rice noodles, with the acidic broth and protein content further attenuating postprandial glucose response. This dish is clinically relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its moderate carbohydrate load and low GI, but portion control is essential given the glycemic load of approximately 23.6 per standard 500 g serving. The dish is dairy-free and rich in B vitamins, iron, and zinc from the beef components. It is a popular comfort food in Thailand and widely served in Thai restaurants across Southeast Asia, including Singapore.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Tom Yum Beef Noodle Soup was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for cooked beef brisket, beef tendon, and rice noodles combined with FAO/INFOODS SEA broth composition data. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for rice noodles as the primary glycemic driver. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central (cooked beef brisket FDC ID 174032, cooked rice noodles FDC ID 168917) and FAO/INFOODS SEA data for Thai spicy broths. No exact match was found in any primary approved source. Sodium is high due to fish sauce and broth concentration; actual restaurant values may vary significantly. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component sources used for this composite estimate. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources for this composite estimate. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources for this composite estimate. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources for this composite estimate. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources for this composite estimate. GI estimated at 45 based on University of Sydney GI Database rice noodle data; no direct GI measurement for Tom Yum Beef Noodle Soup exists. Halal status is unconfirmed for the specific restaurant (Saap Saap Thai - Funan). The dish may be served with either rice noodles or rice per the source description; nutrient values assume rice noodle preparation.
